title: Executable Safety Net
one_liner: Tests, builds, types, lints and policy checks tell the agent — fast — whether its change is good.
problem: Without fast, deterministic feedback, the agent's only validator is human review. Bugs survive because the loop is too slow to catch them; the agent can't tell a regression from a successful change; reviewers become the runtime. The system encourages cautious half-changes that ship anyway.
solution: Make validation runnable on a developer machine and in CI in a single command: unit + integration tests, type checks, lint, format, dependency policy, security scan, smoke build. Optimize for under a few minutes for the agent loop. Tag tests by feature so the agent can run a relevant subset. The output of a successful run is the agent's permission to propose the change for review.
